title: Fix Excel re-import to update records by item ID, rename import/export buttons

requirements.summary: Use item ID as stable key during Excel import: update existing items when ID matches, create new items only for unmatched IDs
acceptanceCriteria: Downloading the inventory as Excel, editing a row's name or price in Excel, then re-uploading the file causes the corresponding item in the app to reflect the edited values.
An item whose ID exists in both the app and the Excel file is updated, not duplicated.
A row in Excel with an ID not found in the app is added as a new item.
Items in the app that have no matching row in the uploaded Excel file remain unchanged.

file_operations.description: Modify the Excel import handler (handleImport function) to treat 'ID Barang' as the stable primary key. When processing each row from the uploaded Excel file, check if an item with matching ID already exists in the current inventory. If found, call the backend mutation to update that item's fields (name, selling price, purchase price, quantity, product type) with values from Excel. If the ID is not found, add it as a new item. Ensure items not present in the Excel file remain unchanged.

requirements.summary: Rename 'Impor Excel' button to 'Unggah' and 'Ekspor Excel' button to 'Unduh' on the Inventory page
acceptanceCriteria: The button previously labeled 'Impor Excel' now displays 'Unggah'.
The button previously labeled 'Ekspor Excel' now displays 'Unduh'.
Both buttons retain their existing functionality.

file_operations.description: Update the button labels in the InventoryPage component: change the 'Impor Excel' button text to 'Unggah' and the 'Ekspor Excel' button text to 'Unduh'. Do not change the onClick handlers or button functionality.
